title: rosa communis var. latebrosa
intro: Rosa communis var. latebrosa is a lesser-known variety of the Rosa communis species, commonly referred to as the common rose or dog rose, with its name suggesting a deeper coloration of its flowers, though this is not a widely used term. As a medicinal plant, Rosa communis var. latebrosa has been used to treat various ailments due to its rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. Rosa communis var. latebrosa is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into herbal remedies and skincare products.
